input:
Suhayl ibn ʿAmr, however, was unmoved. He said to God’s Messenger: “This is the first person whose case I take up. You must return him to me.” The Prophet referred to the fact that Abū Jandal arrived before the agreement was completed: “We have not finished drawing up the document yet.” Suhayl said: “Then I have not agreed any terms with you whatsoever.” The Prophet pleaded: “Then allow me him.” Suhayl said that he would not. Mikraz, another member of the Quraysh delegation, said that they should allow Muhammad to have Abū Jandal.
Source: Adil Salahi, Muhammad: Man and Prophet, Ch. 28: Peace in the Making

output.scene_title: Suhayl Refuses to Release Abū Jandal
output.source_reference: Adil Salahi, Muhammad: Man and Prophet, Ch. 28: Peace in the Making

characters.name: Suhayl ibn ʿAmr
characters.appearance: Early 50s, medium build, confident posture. Dark brown skin, thick beard with slight grey streaks, dark brown eyes. Wearing a dark brown woolen cloak over a white thobe, a white cloth wrapped around his head as a turban. Distinguished by his stern expression and imposing stature.

characters.name: Mikraz ibn ʿAmr
characters.appearance: Early 50s, medium build, dignified posture. Dark brown skin, thick grey-streaked beard, dark brown eyes. Wearing a dark brown woolen cloak over a white thobe, a white cloth wrapped around his head as a turban. Distinguished by his calm demeanor and wise expression.

characters.name: Prophet Muhammad (PBUH)
characters.appearance: Early 50s, medium build, dignified upright posture. Wearing a simple white thobe with a green cloak draped over shoulders, white turban with tail between shoulder blades. Simple leather sandals. Always shown from behind, face never visible. Distinguished by green cloak and subtle warm light.

characters.name: Abū Jandal ibn Hishām
characters.appearance: Late 40s, medium build, slightly disheveled posture. Dark brown skin, short beard with slight brown tint, dark brown eyes. Wearing a simple white thobe, no cloak, a white cloth wrapped loosely around his head. Distinguished by his worried expression and disheveled hair.

output.moral_lesson: Compromise and understanding are key to resolving conflicts and finding peace.
output.narrative_text: Suhayl ibn ʿAmr refused to let the Prophet have Abū Jandal, saying they had not agreed on any terms yet. The Prophet asked Suhayl to reconsider, but Suhayl refused. Another Quraysh delegate, Mikraz, intervened and advised Suhayl to allow the Prophet to have Abū Jandal.

compliance.notes: Prophet shown from behind, Abū Jandal shown with full face, worried expression. Suhayl and Mikraz shown with full faces, serious expressions. No graphic conflict, peaceful scene.

output.image_prompt: Children's watercolor illustration. Exterior of a 7th century Makkah mud-brick home. A dignified man in white thobe and green cloak, white turban, seen entirely from behind, standing at an open doorway with warm golden afternoon sunlight streaming behind him. Nearby, two men in dark brown woolen cloaks over white thobes, dark brown turbans, looking at each other with serious expressions. One of them, Abū Jandal, shown with full face, worried expression, standing slightly apart from the others. Simple mud-brick walls, clay water jug in corner. Medium shot, eye level. Warm golden palette. No modern objects.
